title: Drum and Drumstick (Membranophone)

Simms; Collection Site: North America, Canada, Saskatchewan, Day Star Reserve; Courtesy of Chicago Field Museum

title: Drum and Drumstick (Membranophone)
desc: Drum and stick. Single hand. Half of face of drum is blue (sky), the other half is yellow Earth. Brass beads in front of drum. Buckskin covered stick, used in thunderstorm; Accession Year: 1903; Accession Number: 851; Collected by: Stephen C. Simms; Collection Site: North America, Canada, Saskatchewan, Day Star Reserve; Courtesy of Chicago Field Museum

title: Whistle / Aerophone (Image Not Displayed)

title: Drum
desc: Single hand and half yellow, other half in parallel (13) red stripes. 13 1/2" diameter, 2 1/2" depth; Accession Year: 1903; Accession Number: 851; Collected by: Stephen C. Simms; Collection Site: North America, Canada, Saskatchewan, Day Star Reserve; Courtesy of Chicago Field Museum

title: Drum and Supports
desc: Drum for dances and ceremonies, irregular in shape (28" diameter, 5" thick). Double hand. Painted with blue, red, and yellow for the sun. Simms; Collection Site: North America, Canada, Saskatchewan, Day Star Reserve; Courtesy of Chicago Field Museum
